extracted_items.content: Architecture decision: modular monolith. Extract services only when there is a clear scaling need.
API technology: GraphQL with Apollo Server. Chosen to support multiple clients (web, mobile, third-party integrations).
Authentication: OAuth2 with PKCE for all clients. JWT tokens using RS256 algorithm. No API key authentication for first-party clients.
Search infrastructure: Elasticsearch. Chosen for complex full-text search requirements.
Caching: Redis for session cache and hot data. TTL configuration: 1 hour default for most keys, 24 hours for user profiles.
Background jobs: Celery with Redis broker. Periodic tasks via Celery Beat. No separate job service.
File storage: S3-compatible via Cloudflare R2. CDN via Cloudflare. Chosen to avoid AWS S3 egress costs.
Observability: structured JSON logs shipped to Grafana Loki. Traces via OpenTelemetry to Grafana Tempo. ELK stack rejected as too heavy.
Infrastructure as Code: Terraform for all cloud resources. Manual console changes are not allowed — all infrastructure must be in IaC.
Secrets management: HashiCorp Vault for all secrets. No secrets in environment variables directly. All secrets must be retrieved from Vault. This is non-negotiable.
Error tracking: Sentry integrated across all services.
Code review policy: PRs require 2 approvals. No self-merge on main branch. Main branch is protected. Feature flags required for all risky deploys.
